artPrompt: A vast still reservoir stretches into the frame, its black surface glazed with sickly green bioluminescent scum, ringed by crumbling concrete and rusted pipes half-swallowed by mutant overgrowth, depths plunging unknowably dark below a bruised storm sky. No figures, only the brooding poisoned water. Wide landscape, murky green and rust-red washes bleeding into deep teal. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: The Reservoir Itself
text:
The Reservoir Itself enters the battlefield tapped.
{T}: Add {R} or {G}.
Feed (Whenever a player rolls one or more dice, flips one or more coins, or votes, put a sludge counter on target creature you control.)
{4}, {T}, Remove five sludge counters from among creatures you control: Surface the thing — target creature you control can't be blocked this turn.
flavorText: Surface area: forty acres. Depth: nobody's been brave enough to finish measuring.
